citations.description: Ticket comment by Joe Oakhart (timestamp 1784420495870006001): 'Re-planned R3 off current origin/main... review-plan PASS (blocking:0); claimed. Running bounded sanity eval next...' — no Gerrit submission or vote attestation present.
Ticket status is 'in_progress', not closed, with no further comments attesting Gerrit submission.

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] The change is landed on main: `LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1` and submitted (Gerrit), attested in-session.
findings.detail: The ticket has one in-session comment (by Joe Oakhart) but it contains no attestation of Gerrit submission, no LLM-Review +1/Verified +1 vote record, no change URL, and no submission timestamp. The comment only describes implementation progress ('review-plan PASS (blocking:0); claimed. Running bounded sanity eval next...'). The ticket status remains in_progress. The operator-attested criterion requires at least one verifiable specific — a Gerrit change URL/id, named votes, or submission confirmation — none of which appear anywhere in the ticket.
findings.remediation: Record proof of Gerrit submission as a ticket comment citing the specific Gerrit change URL/id, the observed vote scores (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1), the submitter name, and the submission timestamp.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
